name: Grandma Vanicek's Czechoslovakian Potato Dumpling
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 1
These are not fluffy dumplings as in chicken and dumplings. They are a "sturdy" potato dumpling served as a side dish to meat, and usually with a sweet sauerkraut/carraway/honey dish as the vegetable.
Boil peeled potatoes in small quantity of water. Drain potatoes thoroughly.
Rice potatoes through hand held masher/ricer. Let potatoes cool.
On floured board, gradually add flour while working mixture into dough with hands. Shape potato mixture into a long roll and cut into 10 slices.
Drop dumplings into boiling water and boil gently about 5 minutes.
Drain dumplings on paper towel. The dumplings are fully cooked after the boiling process. Traditionally, Czechoslovakians then melt butter in a heavy frying pan and fry the dumplings until they are lightly browned on all sides.
